patch.value: pm validate: ok:false on warn-only checks + dumps every item ID per field

Dogfood 2026-05-22 HIGH (agent UX + token): on a healthy project, validate returns ok:false purely because warn-level checks fire (closed missing resolution, missing acceptance_criteria/etc under core profile), and lists every affected ID for every field (NxM payload). Agents read ok:false as a hard failure and may abort; the ID lists are very token-heavy. Fix: top-level ok=true when only warn checks fire (separate ok from has_warnings); aggregate/truncate per-field ID lists by default with counts, full lists behind --verbose. Keep the remediation hints.

value.author: codex-validate-worker-20260522
value.text: Implementation plan: separate hard validation failures from warning-only checks so top-level ok stays true for warnings; keep warnings visible via has_warnings/warnings; compact metadata and resolution ID arrays by default while preserving counts and truncation markers; add focused validate regression coverage and run an isolated temp-project validate smoke.

patch.value: Implemented validate warning-only semantics: warnings remain visible through has_warnings/warnings, ok only flips false for explicit error-severity findings, diagnostic ID lists default to compact 40-item summaries with --verbose-diagnostics escape hatch, and --strict-exit/--fail-on-warn preserve warning-fail automation.
